executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: The architecture watchdog artifact drifted from live runtime reality by conflating three persisted disabled historical jobs in jobs.json with the live Gateway cron topology, which had 20 enabled jobs and 0 live-disabled jobs.
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: Keep architecture signoff fail-closed on live-vs-artifact topology mismatches so persisted disabled history can never be reported as live runtime state.

audit_metadata.scheduler_inspection_method: direct openclaw cron list --json plus jobs.json comparison with explicit live-vs-persisted separation

owns: bounded timeout reruns and timeout tuning
artifact contract checks for code-backed shared-market reuse
syncing research artifacts into the git repo

findings.title: Architecture signoff drifted from live cron reality by reporting persisted disabled history as live topology
findings.mechanism: agent_architecture_latest.json claimed live_jobs_disabled=3 and listed three disabled job names even though openclaw cron list --json showed 20 live jobs, 20 enabled, and 0 live-disabled jobs.
findings.root_cause: The architecture path preserved intentional historical disabled jobs from jobs.json but did not keep them clearly separated from live Gateway runtime state in its latest-state artifact.
findings.recommended_fix: Fail closed whenever the architecture audit metadata disagrees with live Gateway cron topology, and report persisted disabled history in separate fields only.

findings.title: The health-monitor and architecture independent-verification boundaries needed stronger topology-coherence enforcement
findings.mechanism: Before this repair, the architecture independent verifier passed without validating that the architecture artifact's live topology metadata matched openclaw cron list --json.
findings.root_cause: Independent signoff covered freshness and peer artifacts but did not yet validate live-vs-artifact scheduler coherence explicitly.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep the new live-topology coherence checks and watchdog instructions in the runtime path so future architecture reports cannot self-certify stale topology wording.

findings.title: Marketing learning is runtime-backed but measurable outcome movement is still absent
findings.mechanism: The latest marketing audit still keeps the bottleneck explicit as distribution_and_message_to_primary_repo_conversion and the independent verification still notes flat Codeberg adoption.
findings.root_cause: The marketing loop now certifies more cleanly than it converts that discipline into adoption gains.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep replacing tactics and architecture until Codeberg adoption moves; do not let measurement-pending status become a resting state.

findings.title: Retired topology remains persisted for audit history but is now clearly separated from live runtime state
findings.mechanism: Three legacy jobs still exist in jobs.json for audit memory, while live runtime ownership remains in Gateway and user crontab stays clean.
findings.root_cause: Historical retention is intentional and safe only when it stays explicitly non-authoritative for live runtime claims.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep legacy jobs disabled, clearly labeled as historical, and excluded from live topology counts.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Keep architecture signoff bound to live Gateway cron topology rather than persisted scheduler history
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: This was the live contradiction that made the previous architecture artifact untrustworthy.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: The architecture watchdog and health monitor fail closed before stale topology wording can be treated as healthy.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Preserve independent verification of the repaired architecture and health-monitor boundaries
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: The repair touched verification-bearing runtime code and needed fresh proof.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Future health runs catch topology/report drift directly instead of trusting stale pass artifacts.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Convert marketing self-improvement into measurable Codeberg adoption movement
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: The strongest remaining loop risk is still outcome stagnation rather than certifiability drift.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: The system keeps rewarding real distribution outcomes instead of activity-only marketing work.

repairs_applied_this_run.change: Added direct architecture live-topology coherence checks so health monitoring now fails closed when agent_architecture_latest.json disagrees with openclaw cron list --json.
Added independent live-vs-artifact Gateway cron topology checks so architecture signoff now fails closed on topology drift.
Clarified that openclaw cron list --json is the live topology source of truth and persisted disabled jobs in jobs.json must be reported separately.
Refreshed the architecture artifact to match current live topology: 20 live jobs, 20 enabled, 0 live-disabled, with historical disabled jobs separated into persisted-history fields.

independent_verification.summary: Independent verification now confirms that the refreshed architecture artifact matches live Gateway cron topology, the architecture verifier fails closed on stale signoff, and the repaired health-monitor boundary is green.

highest_risk_unresolved_issue.title: Marketing outcomes remain flat despite better loop discipline
highest_risk_unresolved_issue.why: The loop now certifies more defensibly, but Codeberg adoption and broader distribution results have still not moved in the current window.
